We'll mainly use the Requests and BeautifulSoup packages to build a few things. As for what those things are, let's just look at the requirements:

returns a list of HTML elements, so we use a for loop to pull out the topic, the link, and the contestant count. We'll need a variable to hold the cleaned-up data. Even though we've already narrowed down to a specific block, to be more precise we use BeautifulSoup's

Finally, use the JSON module to save the
Here's the full example for reference:

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import json

r = requests.get('https://ithelp.ithome.com.tw/2021ironman/signup/list')

if r.status_code == 200:
  soup = BeautifulSoup(r.text, 'lxml')
  tags = soup.select('body div.wrapper div.border-frame.clearfix div.contestants-nav ul.list-unstyled.contestants-nav__ul li.contestants-nav__item a.group-nav')
  cacheData = []
  for item in tags:
    if item.find('div', { "class": "group-nav__text"}).text.strip() != "全部參賽鐵人":
      cacheData.append({
        'name': item.find('div', { "class": "group-nav__text"}).text.strip(),
        'num': item.find('div', { "class": "group-nav__num"}).text.strip(),
        'url': item.get('href', None)
      })
  f = open('menu.json', 'w')
  f.write(json.dumps(cacheData))
  f.close()
tags = soup.select('body div.wrapper div.border-frame.clearfix div.contestants-nav ul.list-unstyled.contestants-nav__ul li.contestants-nav__item a.group-nav')
Grabbing each topic's contestant links and titles
We've finished pulling the topic list, so next up is grabbing the contestant entries inside each topic.
We just built a menu.json, so we'll use that data to request each contestant's title and link. Start by creating a getUserData.py file and importing the packages we'll need:

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import json
function to open menu.json. One thing to watch out for: after reading the JSON file, remember to parse it back into a dictionary, or you'll hit an error:
Then we use a for loop with requests to fetch each topic's page:

for item in data:
  r = requests.get(item['url'])

At this point you'll probably notice something: we only get the first page of each topic's contestant list, but not the rest of the pages. If you scroll down the topic list, you'll see the pagination buttons:

So you might be wondering — we've only got the first page of each topic, how do we get the rest? It's actually pretty simple. Go to a topic list, count roughly how many entries are on one page, then use the contestant count we cleaned up earlier to work out how many pages there are. Pair that with a loop and you can make multiple requests. Enough talk, here's the code:

With this approach you can pull back every page of data. After that it's just organizing each topic's data into a new structure and saving it to its own JSON file. The logic is similar to before, so let's jump straight to the full example:

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import json
import math

f = open('menu.json', 'r')
data = json.loads(f.read())

for item in data:
  pages = math.ceil(int(item['num']) / 10)
  cacheData = []
  for page in range(pages):
    r = requests.get(f'{item["url"]}&page={page + 1}')
    if r.status_code == 200:
      soup = BeautifulSoup(r.text, 'lxml')
      tags = soup.select('body div.wrapper div.border-frame.clearfix div.contestants-wrapper div.contestants-list.clearfix')
      for tag in tags:
        cacheData.append({
          'id': tag.find('a', { "class": "contestants-expect"}).get('data-id', None),
          'name': tag.find('div', { "class": "contestants-list__name"}).text.strip(),
          'title': tag.find('a', { "class": "contestants-list__title"}).text.strip(),
          'url': tag.find('a', { "class": "contestants-list__title"}).get('href', None),
          'like': tag.find('span', { "class": "contestants-expect__number"}).text.strip()
        })
  print(f'已經取得 {item["name"]} 主題，共 {len(cacheData)} 參賽資料。')
  f = open(f'./data/{item["name"]}.json', 'a')
  f.write(json.dumps(cacheData))
  f.close()
tags = soup.select('body div.wrapper div.border-frame.clearfix div.contestants-wrapper div.contestants-list.clearfix')
print(f'已經取得 {item["name"]} 主題，共 {len(cacheData)} 參賽資料。')

And that's a complete scraping example. You could of course take it further — pull each topic's subscriber count, calculate total view counts, and other stats the Ironman site doesn't hand you directly. I'll leave that part as your final homework.
